I thank the Lord for those within our congregation that use their spiritual gifts to not only bring glory to God, but to edify the believers. And we make ourselves available. We seize the opportunities. Why? Because I want to use it. You know, it's really difficult for me. There's been times in which I've had super busy times and my schedule is seeming always busy. But someone, hey, will you preach this meeting or will you do this? It's hard for me to say no. Why? Because God gave me a gift and I want to be able to use it. Listen, I don't want to be in a place where we don't have church services because, well, you know, things are changing and we're just not having night services or this. No, I know so many people that are canceling church services and here we are adding one on Wednesday morning. It's a situation where what I'm saying is that when you know what it is and you've developed it, you ought to long to use it. It's not to be stored up. It's not something that we just kind of keep in a box somewhere. It's something that we use for God's glory. And when we do so, not only will God be glorified, not only will the believers be edified, but I truly believe this, you'll have joy. You'll have joy. We sing the song, there is joy in serving Jesus. It doesn't mean that it's not work. Serving the Lord is work. Amen? And some people have asked me over the years and some people that have taught and preached in my absence have commented on how challenging it is to, how can you develop so many messages a week, week after week after week? Listen, it's labor, but it's joy. I'm telling you, it's joy. I absolutely love it. Why? Because I know that this is what God equipped me to do. I know that it's what God gifted me to do. And I don't say that pridefully, I say that humbly, that to God be the glory. I want to do it for God's glory and I love doing it. Is it labor? Is it work? Absolutely. But I promise you, you'll have joy because there's joy in serving Jesus. We understand that the spiritual gifts as they've been described, defined in God's Word, and I say, I want to now discover what is my spiritual gift. And I want to develop this. Why? Because I want to use it. I want to be the best that I can be, not so that anyone would look at me and say, they should be lifted up. But also that people would say, this is God's grace. That God would be glorified and then deploy it, use it for God's glory. Look for those opportunities, seize those opportunities to be used for God's glory. Let's pray. Father, we love you. We thank you so much for loving us. We thank you for the spiritual gift, Lord, that you've given to each of us. We thank you for the truth of your Word. And I pray that you would help us as your people, Lord, to truly desire to know what our spiritual gift is, that God, you would reveal it to us. If maybe there's some here tonight that just don't know what their spiritual gift is, God, I pray that you would just lay upon their heart a desire to know it, Lord, to be able to discover what their spiritual gift is. Maybe there's some that know what their spiritual gift is, but they're just simply not using it, God, at this time for your glory. I pray that you'd stir us, Lord. Help us, even as Paul instructs Timothy, to stir up the gift that is in us, to rekindle, to redetermine that we will use the gift that you've bestowed upon us for your glory, that we would be good stewards of the manifold grace of God. I pray that in the moments of invitation that you'd have your will and way, that you would work in hearts and lives as only you can, and we'll thank you for it in Christ's name. We pray.
